title: Why I leave one afternoon a week with no plan
summary: Argue for the deliberate practice of leaving one afternoon a week unplanned, and what it gives back that no scheduled activity can.
prompt: Write 3 paragraphs (350–500 words) arguing for the deliberate practice of leaving one afternoon a week without any plan, and what it gives back that scheduled time cannot. Use the present perfect for accumulated experience, a first conditional, modals, and 'however' or 'although'.
hintStarters: For the past four years, on every Thursday afternoon I happen to be in Lyon, I have refused to put anything in my calendar between two and six, and at first my colleagues assumed…
If you tell a typical professional adult that they should keep one afternoon a week completely unplanned, they will often ask, with genuine puzzlement, what is supposed to happen in it, because…

Although doing nothing in particular sounds like a luxury that only the unemployed can afford, what I have come to think after four years of protecting these afternoons is that they are not really doing nothing — they are…

hintVocab.meaningVi: để thời gian không có kế hoạch
đi lang thang không mục tiêu
một buổi chiều không hiệu quả
đi theo sự tò mò của mình
nghỉ ngơi như là công việc
bảo vệ thời gian của mình
đặt câu hỏi về văn hóa năng suất

sampleText:
For the past four years, on every Thursday afternoon I happen to be in Lyon, I have refused to put anything in my calendar between two and six. At first my colleagues assumed I was using the time for a specific project. Later they assumed it was a doctor's appointment, then a class, then a quiet job interview. The truth, which embarrassed me to admit for the first year, is that I do nothing in particular. I might walk along the river. I might sit in a café and read. I might do laundry, take a nap, or buy fruit at a market I rarely visit. The afternoon has no theme. I do not pre-decide what will happen in it. By Wednesday evening I have already noticed that I am protecting it; by Friday morning I have already noticed that I needed it more than I had thought.

If you tell a typical professional adult that they should keep one afternoon a week completely unplanned, they will often ask, with genuine puzzlement, what is supposed to happen in it. The question itself reveals the culture we live inside. Time, in the world most of us work in, is either spent on a defined activity or treated as wasted. The category of 'time that is allowed to be undecided' has almost disappeared from the calendar of an average city adult. Although filling every hour with deliberate activity feels efficient, what it actually does is leave no room for the kind of slow noticing that produces new ideas, repaired moods, and the small repairs to one's life that nobody ever schedules. The afternoons I have left empty have given me more useful thoughts about my work, my friendships, and what I want the next decade to look like than any scheduled 'thinking time' ever has.

However, I do not want to argue that unplanned time is a kind of magic. Some people, given a completely free afternoon, will simply work; others will spiral into anxious checking of their phones. The discipline is not 'do nothing'; it is 'do not pre-decide'. You let the afternoon tell you what it wants. Some weeks it wants a long walk, others a museum, others a long bath. Although employers often pay lip service to rest, the calendar tells the truth: an organisation that treats every hour as billable is teaching its staff that unplanned time is suspicious. I cannot change that culture on my own. What I can do is protect one afternoon a week from it, and after four years I am sure of two things — I do less obviously useful work in those four hours, and I am noticeably more useful in the other thirty-six because of them.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: First conditional for predicting a typical response
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'If you tell a typical professional adult that they should keep one afternoon a week completely unplanned, they will often ask…' là điều kiện loại 1 — hiện tại đơn ở mệnh đề if, 'will + nguyên thể' ở mệnh đề chính — dùng để dự đoán phản ứng có khả năng xảy ra.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'If you tell a typical professional adult that they should keep one afternoon a week completely unplanned, they will often ask…' is the first conditional — present simple in the if-clause, 'will + base verb' in the main — used to predict a likely response.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'Either … or' for two contrasting options
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Time, in the world most of us work in, is either spent on a defined activity or treated as wasted' dùng cấu trúc 'either X or Y' với hai phân từ quá khứ (spent, treated) song song — một cách thanh lịch để mô tả một sự lưỡng phân chỉ có hai lựa chọn.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Time, in the world most of us work in, is either spent on a defined activity or treated as wasted' uses the 'either X or Y' structure with two parallel past participles (spent, treated) — an elegant way to describe a binary with only two options.

sampleTranslationVi:
Trong bốn năm qua, vào mỗi chiều thứ Năm tôi tình cờ ở Lyon, tôi đã từ chối đặt bất cứ thứ gì vào lịch giữa hai và sáu giờ. Lúc đầu các đồng nghiệp của tôi cho rằng tôi đang dùng thời gian đó cho một dự án cụ thể. Sau đó họ cho rằng đó là một cuộc hẹn với bác sĩ, rồi một lớp học, rồi một cuộc phỏng vấn việc làm lặng lẽ. Sự thật, điều khiến tôi xấu hổ khi thừa nhận trong năm đầu tiên, là tôi không làm gì cụ thể. Tôi có thể đi dọc bờ sông. Tôi có thể ngồi trong một quán cà phê và đọc sách. Tôi có thể giặt giũ, ngủ trưa, hoặc mua trái cây ở một chợ tôi hiếm khi ghé thăm. Buổi chiều không có chủ đề. Tôi không quyết định trước điều gì sẽ xảy ra trong nó. Đến tối thứ Tư tôi đã nhận ra rằng tôi đang bảo vệ nó; đến sáng thứ Sáu tôi đã nhận ra rằng tôi cần nó nhiều hơn tôi đã nghĩ.

Nếu bạn nói với một người lớn chuyên nghiệp điển hình rằng họ nên giữ một buổi chiều mỗi tuần hoàn toàn không có kế hoạch, họ thường sẽ hỏi, với sự bối rối thực sự, điều gì được cho là sẽ xảy ra trong đó. Bản thân câu hỏi tiết lộ văn hóa chúng ta đang sống bên trong. Thời gian, trong thế giới hầu hết chúng ta làm việc trong, hoặc được dành cho một hoạt động xác định hoặc bị coi là lãng phí. Loại 'thời gian được phép không xác định' gần như đã biến mất khỏi lịch của một người lớn thành phố trung bình. Mặc dù việc lấp đầy mỗi giờ bằng hoạt động có chủ ý cảm thấy hiệu quả, điều nó thực sự làm là không để chỗ cho loại nhận ra chậm tạo ra ý tưởng mới, tâm trạng được sửa chữa, và những sửa chữa nhỏ cho cuộc đời mà không ai từng lên lịch. Những buổi chiều tôi đã để trống đã cho tôi nhiều suy nghĩ hữu ích hơn về công việc, tình bạn, và thập kỷ tới tôi muốn trông như thế nào, so với bất kỳ 'thời gian suy nghĩ' có lịch nào từng cho tôi.

Tuy nhiên, tôi không muốn lập luận rằng thời gian không có kế hoạch là một loại phép thuật. Một số người, khi được một buổi chiều hoàn toàn rảnh, sẽ chỉ làm việc; những người khác sẽ rơi vào việc kiểm tra điện thoại đầy lo lắng. Kỷ luật không phải là 'không làm gì'; nó là 'không quyết định trước'. Bạn để buổi chiều nói cho bạn biết nó muốn gì. Một số tuần nó muốn một chuyến đi dạo dài, những tuần khác là một bảo tàng, những tuần khác là một bồn tắm dài. Mặc dù chủ lao động thường nói suông về sự nghỉ ngơi, lịch cho biết sự thật: một tổ chức đối xử với mỗi giờ như có thể tính phí đang dạy nhân viên rằng thời gian không có kế hoạch là đáng ngờ. Tôi không thể thay đổi văn hóa đó một mình. Điều tôi có thể làm là bảo vệ một buổi chiều mỗi tuần khỏi nó, và sau bốn năm tôi chắc chắn về hai điều — tôi làm ít công việc hữu ích rõ rệt hơn trong bốn giờ đó, và tôi rõ rệt hữu ích hơn trong ba mươi sáu giờ còn lại nhờ chúng.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses present perfect correctly?
options: For the past four years, I have refuse to put anything in my calendar between two and six.
For the past four years, I refused to put anything in my calendar between two and six.
For the past four years, I have refused to put anything in my calendar between two and six.
For the past four years, I have been refuse to put anything in my calendar between two and six.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present perfect with 'for the past four years' = 'have + past participle' ('refused').

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses the first conditional correctly?
options: If you will tell a typical professional adult, they will often ask what is supposed to happen.
If you tell a typical professional adult, they would often ask what is supposed to happen.
If you told a typical professional adult, they will often ask what is supposed to happen.
If you tell a typical professional adult, they will often ask what is supposed to happen.
mcQuestions.explanation: First conditional: present simple in the if-clause + 'will + base verb' in the main clause. Avoid 'will' in the if-clause.

options: Although filling every hour with deliberate activity feels efficient, it leaves no room for slow noticing.
Although filling every hour with deliberate activity feels efficient, but it leaves no room for slow noticing.
Although, filling every hour with deliberate activity feels efficient it leaves no room for slow noticing.
Filling every hour with deliberate activity feels efficient although however it leaves no room for slow noticing.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Although + clause, main clause' — no 'but', no comma after 'although', no doubling.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'however' correctly to introduce a contrast?
options: However I do not want to argue that unplanned time is a kind of magic.
However, I do not want to argue that unplanned time is a kind of magic.
However; I do not want to argue that unplanned time is a kind of magic.
However, I do not want to arguing that unplanned time is a kind of magic.
mcQuestions.explanation: Sentence-initial 'however' takes a comma. After 'want', use 'to + base verb' ('to argue').

options: Time is either spent on a defined activity nor treated as wasted.
Time is either spent on a defined activity and treated as wasted.
Time is either spent on a defined activity or treated as wasted.
Time is both spent on a defined activity or treated as wasted.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Either X or Y' is the correct correlative pair; 'nor' goes with 'neither'; 'and' is wrong with 'either'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'might + base verb' for possibility correctly?
options: I might walking along the river. I might sitting in a café and read.
I might to walk along the river. I might to sit in a café and read.
I might walked along the river. I might sat in a café and read.
I might walk along the river. I might sit in a café and read.
mcQuestions.explanation: Modal 'might' + bare infinitive (base verb). Never '-ing', 'to', or past form after the modal.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'be supposed to + base verb' correctly?
options: They will often ask what is supposed to happen in it.
They will often ask what is supposed to happens in it.
They will often ask what is suppose to happen in it.
They will often ask what is supposed happening in it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Be supposed to + base verb' is the standard structure; 'supposed' (past participle) and bare infinitive 'happen'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option correctly uses 'the kind of + noun + that + clause'?
options: The kind of slow noticing what produces new ideas.
The kind of slow noticing that produces new ideas.
The kind of slow noticing which it produces new ideas.
The kind of slow noticing produces new ideas.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'The kind of + noun + that/which + verb' is the standard structure. 'What' is wrong as a relative; do not repeat the subject 'it'.

options: I am noticeable more useful in the other thirty-six hours because of them.
I am noticeably most useful in the other thirty-six hours because of them.
I am noticeably more useful in the other thirty-six hours because of them.
I am noticeably more useful in the other thirty-six hours because for them.
mcQuestions.explanation: Adverb 'noticeably' modifies the comparative 'more useful'. 'Most' is superlative; 'because of + noun' is the correct preposition.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses past perfect correctly?
options: I needed it more than I have thought.
I needed it more than I had think.
I needed it more than I had been thinking.
I needed it more than I had thought.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect = 'had + past participle' ('thought') for an earlier past belief compared to the main past moment.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'given + noun' as a participial clause correctly?
options: Some people, given a completely free afternoon, will simply work.
Some people, giving a completely free afternoon, will simply work.
Some people, gave a completely free afternoon, will simply work.
Some people, given of a completely free afternoon, will simply work.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Given + noun' is the past-participle clause meaning 'if they are given'. 'Giving' (active -ing) would mean they do the giving.

criticalThinkingQuestion: If unplanned time is genuinely valuable, why is it almost never modelled or rewarded in modern workplaces? Could the cultural pressure to fill every hour itself be a form of damage that organisations should treat as seriously as overtime?
